Dear Ms. Ramirez,

Thank you again for meeting with Ethan yesterday about his schedule and stress levels. He came home saying he felt a lot better after talking with you about balancing band, robotics, and soccer with his homework.

You also mentioned that the school prefers to have updated background notes on file for students who’ve had anxiety around testing. If you need anything from us—like the name of Ethan’s pediatrician or more information about how he reacted during last spring’s standardized tests—please let me know. I’m happy to fill out any forms or send an email summary so it can be attached to his student record.

Ethan is currently in 7th grade and overall doing well, but we really appreciate the extra support to make sure he doesn’t get overwhelmed this year. Best,

Melissa A. Carlson
7426 Willow Bend Drive
Woodbury, MN 55129

Hello,

The instructions say to enter the date the information is being submitted, but the example screenshot on the portal shows a past date of 2025-08-27, which was before school even started for us. I just want to confirm that I should use today’s date (2025-12-03) rather than the first day of the 2025–2026 school year.

I’m also wondering if this is supposed to match the date we signed the paper emergency contact sheet that came home in Ethan’s backpack back in September. That form was signed on 2025-09-09, and I don’t want to create any discrepancy between the digital and paper records.

We’ve been more careful with all these forms this year because of how stressed Ethan was during the big testing periods the last couple of springs. We know the school strongly encourages participation, but we’re refusing mainly to send a message that we’re fed up with all the testing and the way the district handles it, so I don’t want anything to look incomplete or incorrect.

Thank you,

Melissa A. Carlson

From: registrar@lakeviewms.isd833.org
To: ethan.carlson.family@gmail.com

Subject: Incomplete Student Information – Action Needed for Ethan Carlson

Dear Parent/Guardian of Ethan J. Carlson,

Our records indicate that the online Student Information Update for the 2025–2026 school year is still missing one required item for Ethan.

Specifically, the field labeled “student information > date” has been left blank or contains an invalid entry. This field should reflect the date on which you completed the online verification for Ethan’s record this school year. Some families choose to use the date they first logged in; others use the date they finalized all updates. Either option is acceptable as long as it falls within the current school year (between 2025-08-01 and 2026-06-15).

Examples of acceptable entries include:
- 2025-09-01 (if you completed the form at the start of the year)
- 2025-10-12 (if you updated information after a change in phone number)
- 2025-11-18 (if you are completing the form today)

Please log into the ParentVUE portal, navigate to Ethan’s profile, and enter a valid date in the “student information > date” field no later than 2025-12-05. If you have already done so within this range, you may disregard this message.

We appreciate your prompt attention to this matter so that Ethan’s records remain accurate and up to date.

Sincerely,

Lakeview Middle School Registrar
ISD 833 – South Washington County Schools

Subject: Opt-Out Request for Ethan Carlson – Spring Benchmark Testing

Dear Assessment Coordinator,

I’m writing to request that my son, Ethan J. Carlson, be excused from the upcoming spring benchmark and practice standardized testing days at Lakeview Middle School.

Student information is as follows:
- Name: Ethan James Carlson
- Grade: 7th grade (Lakeview Middle School)
- Homeroom/Advisory Teacher: Ms. Lauren Singh
- Date of Birth: 2012-09-18

Over the past couple of years, we’ve noticed that these extended testing periods significantly increase Ethan’s anxiety and disrupt his sleep and appetite for days beforehand. His teachers already use regular classwork, projects, and quizzes to monitor his progress, and we feel that is sufficient for our family.

To be completely transparent, we’re also looking at that week as a chance to get away for a few days. Honestly, part of why we’re opting Ethan out is that it’s easier for us to take a family trip that week if we don’t have to worry about the testing schedule. We’re hoping a short break will help him reset before the final push of the school year.

Please let us know if there are any additional forms we need to complete, or if you require a written signature. I’m happy to drop something off at the office or reply with an electronic signature if that’s acceptable.

You can reach me at this email address or on my cell at (651) 555-0134.

Thank you for your understanding and for all you do for the students.

Sincerely,

Melissa A. Carlson
7426 Willow Bend Drive

Title: Ethan’s Birthday Party – Trampoline Zone
Date: 2025-09-13 13:00–16:00
Location: SkyBounce Trampoline Zone, Woodbury
Attendees: Ethan, Abby, Mom (Melissa), Dad (Dan), 10–12 classmates from Lakeview, a few soccer teammates

Notes:

Reservation for Ethan’s 13th birthday celebration. Party room booked from 1:00–3:00 p.m., jumping time 1:00–2:30, pizza and cake 2:30–3:00, then cleanup and gift loading 3:00–3:30. We scheduled it for the Saturday *before* his actual birthday so it doesn’t conflict with weekday homework and soccer practice.

Melissa to order a sheet cake with “Happy 13th Birthday Ethan!” written in blue and green frosting. No theme based on a specific video game to avoid licensing designs, but we’ll do generic “gamer” decorations. Ask bakery to avoid peanuts due to one of Ethan’s classmates having a severe allergy.

Remind guests that this is a drop‑off party; parents can leave kids after signing waivers. Put on invitations that Ethan is turning 13 in mid‑September and that he’s in 7th grade at Lakeview Middle School.

Bring: signed waivers, water bottles, extra socks, small gift bags, and a backup inhaler just in case Ethan gets wheezy from all the jumping (he hasn’t needed it in over a year, but better safe). Dan to handle photos and video.

Follow‑up: On his *actual* birthday, we’ll just do family dinner at home and let him choose takeout and a movie.

artifacts.content:
From: Pediatric Associates of Woodbury <noreply@pawclinic.com>
To: Melissa Carlson <melissa.carlson@example.com>
Date: 2024-08-21 09:06
Subject: Immunization Record & School Form for Ethan

Dear Melissa A. Carlson,

Attached please find Ethan J. Carlson’s updated immunization record and the completed “Student Health Summary” form requested by Lakeview Middle School for the 2024–2025 school year.

For privacy, the full date of birth is masked on the form we email, but it will appear in full on the version we fax directly to the school nurse. On your copy, you will see Ethan listed as:

Name: Carlson, Ethan J.
DOB: 09/**/2012 (September, 2012)

Please verify that the month and year match your records. If the day is incorrect on the school’s side, they may have used a placeholder such as 09/01/2012 when he was first entered into their system. This sometimes happens when parents leave the date blank on early intake forms or when forms are transcribed from handwritten notes.

If any of the information on the attached record is inaccurate, reply to this email or call our office so we can correct Ethan’s chart. Note that incorrect birthdates can delay sports physical clearance and participation in activities such as soccer and school band.

As a reminder, Ethan is due for his next well‑child visit in late August 2025, around his 13th birthday. We recommend scheduling early, as our appointments fill quickly before the school year begins.

Sincerely,
Pediatric Associates of Woodbury

artifacts.content:
Title: ParentVUE Data Check – Ethan & Abby
Date: 2025-08-10 20:00–20:45
Location: Home office desk
Attendees: Melissa, Dan

Notes:

Goal: Go through both kids’ online school accounts and make sure all the basic info is correct before the first‑day chaos. For Ethan, double‑check the “Student Information” section, especially the “Student’s Date of Birth” field and emergency contacts.

Last year, ISD 833 somehow had Ethan’s birthday off by a few days in the transportation system, which messed up his bus eligibility report. Dan thinks the system had him listed as 09/10/2012 or maybe 09/01/2012—something close but not actually right. Transportation said it came from the original elementary enrollment file.

Action items:
- Compare what ParentVUE shows for Ethan’s birthdate with what’s on his passport and the pediatrician’s portal.
- Make sure his grade level is 7 and school is Lakeview Middle.
- Confirm Abby’s info at Oak Meadow, including her birth year (2015) and homeroom teacher.
- Update any medical notes about Ethan’s past test‑related anxiety so teachers know to watch for stress.

Melissa to submit any corrections online and upload documents if needed. Dan will email the Lakeview office if the date of birth still looks wrong or if the system won’t let us change it.

Reminder: Once everything is accurate, print a copy of the confirmation screens for our records and put them in the blue school folder in the kitchen drawer.

From: Melissa Carlson <melissa.carlson@email.com>
To: Enrollment Office <enroll@isd833.org>
Date: 2025-01-06 09:14
Subject: Clarification on Ethan Carlson’s Birth Information

Dear Enrollment Team,

I’m completing the online verification for Ethan J. Carlson (Lakeview Middle School, 7th grade) and had a question about the student information section, specifically the date-of-birth field.

When I log in, the system shows his age as 12, which is correct for this school year, but the date listed in the summary screen looks off by a year. I don’t want to accidentally overwrite anything with the wrong date. On a couple of older forms we submitted to Oak Meadow Elementary, his birth year was accidentally written as 2011 because of a typo, and I’m wondering if that’s what’s still in your records.

I don’t see a way to view the exact date you currently have on file—only his age and grade—so I’m hesitant to retype it in case I introduce a new error. Could you please confirm what you currently have recorded for his date of birth and whether you need me to upload his birth certificate again? We did that when he started kindergarten, so I’m hoping it’s still on file.

Also, while I’m writing, I want to be sure his health and mental health history is properly noted. Another reason we’re refusing additional standardized testing this spring is that in March 2024 Ethan started weekly sessions at Nystrom & Associates in Woodbury for test-related anxiety after a crisis visit to Regions Hospital’s pediatric ER on March 3, 2024, when he had chest pain the night before MCA practice tests and they ran an EKG to rule out cardiac issues.

Thank you for helping us get his records straight.

Sincerely,
Melissa A. Carlson
(Mom to Ethan, Grade 7)

artifacts.content:
From: Coach Ramirez <u13soccer@woodburysc.org>
To: Dan and Melissa Carlson <dcarlsonfamily@email.com>
Date: 2024-11-02 18:57
Subject: Age Group Confirmation for Ethan – Spring 2025 Season

Hi Dan and Melissa,

I’m finalizing the roster for the Woodbury SC U13 boys team for the spring 2025 season and wanted to double-check Ethan’s age group placement. The registration system imported his info from last year and currently lists:

Name: Ethan J. Carlson  
Age on 5/1/2025: 13  
Birth Year: 2011

This doesn’t quite match what I remember from talking with Dan on the sidelines, where I thought you mentioned he’d still be 12 for most of the spring. It’s possible that when you first signed him up for rec league a few years ago, the birth year was entered as 2011 instead of 2012, and that error has just followed him through the system.

Normally we place kids strictly by birth year for league play, so the difference between 2011 and 2012 matters for which bracket he’s in. Before I submit our official roster to the league, can you confirm whether the 2011 birth year is correct or if we should have him listed in the younger birth-year group instead? I don’t need the exact date of birth in this email, just the correct year so I can fix it in GotSport.

Also, thanks again for letting me know about his test-related anxiety and the weekly sessions he started at Nystrom & Associates after that scare at Regions Hospital last March. I’ll keep that in mind when we schedule early morning games so he’s not overloaded after a tough school week.

Best,
Coach R.

metadata.subject: Age Group Confirmation for Ethan – Spring 2025 Season

artifacts.content:
From: Grandma Linda <lindapeterson@example.com>
To: Melissa Carlson <melissa.carlson@email.com>
Date: 2025-08-30 07:42
Subject: Birthday ideas for Ethan turning 13

Morning Mel,

I can’t believe our Ethan is going to be a teenager already! It feels like just yesterday I was holding him in the hospital, and now he’s starting 7th grade and talking about robots and soccer strategies.

I know his actual birthday falls sometime in the early fall, but with everyone’s schedules (your work, Dan’s school calendar, Abby’s activities, and Ethan’s soccer and band), I wondered if we should celebrate the weekend before or after. If he’ll be 13 this fall, that would mean he was born in 2012, right? I always mix up whether it was late summer or early fall, and I don’t want to write the wrong thing in his card like I did a few years ago when I accidentally put “Happy 10th on 9/17!” and you teased me that I was off by a day.

Let me know what date works best for a little family party—maybe we can drive down from Maplewood, take him out for pizza in Woodbury, and then he can show us some of his robotics projects. I’ll also bring the photo album from when he was a newborn; I’m sure he’ll pretend to be embarrassed, but he’ll secretly like it.

Love you all,
Mom (Grandma Linda)

From: Melissa Carlson <melissa.carlson@email.com>
To: enrollment@isd833.org
Date: August 5, 2025 9:14 AM
Subject: Clarification on Ethan Carlson’s enrollment and school assignment

Hello,

I’m writing to confirm that we have Ethan J. Carlson enrolled correctly for 7th grade for the 2025–2026 school year. We completed the online registration under South Washington County Schools and selected Lakeview Middle School as his building, since we live at 7426 Willow Bend Drive in Woodbury.

However, when I was poking around the parent portal, I noticed a confusing drop-down that listed several other district schools (Woodbury Middle School, Cottage Grove Middle, Oltman, etc.), and I want to make sure nothing got switched by mistake. Ethan has been attending Lakeview since 6th grade and rides the bus from our neighborhood, so we definitely want him to stay at his current South Washington County school rather than being reassigned somewhere else.

We are also planning to submit an assessment opt-out form again this year. We are opting Ethan out because last spring (April–May 2023) the pressure around MCA scores contributed to him starting on sertraline for anxiety and depression through Dr. Michael Nguyen at the M Health Fairview clinic in Woodbury, and we do not want to risk another medication adjustment or dosage increase just because of standardized test stress.

Could you please confirm that his 2025–2026 enrollment is set to South Washington County Schools – Lakeview Middle School and that no transfer to any other district school has been initiated?

Thank you,
Melissa Carlson
(mom to Ethan, grade 7)

metadata.subject: Clarification on Ethan Carlson’s enrollment and school assignment

Title: ISD 833 Middle School Information Night
Date: January 16, 2025
Time: 6:30 PM – 8:00 PM
Location: Lakeview Middle School Auditorium, Woodbury, MN
Attendees: Melissa Carlson, Ethan Carlson

Description:

District-wide information night for families of current 6th and 7th graders in South Washington County Schools. Principal from Lakeview Middle School will give an overview of course registration, electives (band, choir, robotics, world languages), and support services.

Agenda:
- 6:30–6:45 PM: Welcome from ISD 833 secondary education director
- 6:45–7:10 PM: Lakeview Middle School presentation on daily schedule, advisory, and homework expectations
- 7:10–7:30 PM: Panel with counselors from Lake, Woodbury Middle, and Oltman Middle about transitions and mental health supports
- 7:30–8:00 PM: Breakout sessions for families zoned to different middle schools in the district

Notes:
- Bring Ethan so he can ask about continuing in band (clarinet) and joining the robotics club again next year.
- Ask counselor about how to document his testing opt-out in the student information system so it’s clear for all South Washington County Schools buildings. We are opting him out of state assessments because the MCA pressure in spring 2023 escalated his anxiety to the point that his pediatrician started him on sertraline, and we don’t want a repeat.
- Double-check bus route for our neighborhood to confirm it still lists Lakeview as his assigned ISD 833 school for 7th and 8th grade.

artifacts.content:
From: registrar@lakeviewms.isd833.org
To: melissa.carlson@email.com
Date: May 22, 2025 2:47 PM
Subject: Verification of Resident School – Ethan Carlson (ID# 774392)

Dear Ms. Carlson,

As part of our annual enrollment audit for South Washington County Schools (ISD 833), we are verifying that students are attending their correct resident school based on home address.

Our records show the following for your child:

Student Name: Ethan J. Carlson
Grade (2024–2025): 7
Resident District: South Washington County Schools (ISD 833)
Assigned Middle School: Lakeview Middle School, Woodbury
Home Address: 7426 Willow Bend Drive, Woodbury, MN 55129

If Ethan is currently attending Lakeview Middle School, no action is required. If he is attending another ISD 833 middle school (e.g., Woodbury Middle School, Oltman Middle School, Cottage Grove Middle School) under an approved in-district transfer, please reply with the name of the school so we can ensure his student information is coded correctly.

This verification is important for transportation routing, state reporting, and assessment records. We understand that some families have chosen to opt their children out of certain standardized tests; that choice does not affect the student’s resident school assignment. Any opt-out documentation, such as the form you submitted last year noting that the MCA pressure contributed to Ethan beginning sertraline treatment, will remain in his file regardless of which ISD 833 school he attends.

If any of the above information is inaccurate, please contact our office by June 5 so we can make corrections before the 2025–2026 schedule is finalized.

Sincerely,

Kara Jensen
Registrar
Lakeview Middle School – ISD 833

metadata.subject: Verification of Resident School – Ethan Carlson (ID# 774392)

artifacts.content:
From: Dan Carlson <dcarlson@eastridgehs.org>
To: schoolcounselor@lakeviewms.isd833.org
Date: March 3, 2025 7:58 AM
Subject: Question about transfer options within ISD 833 for Ethan

Hi Ms. Larson,

I’m Ethan Carlson’s dad (I teach social studies over at East Ridge). Ethan is currently a 7th grader at Lakeview Middle School, and overall it’s been a good fit. He likes his English class and the robotics club, and we really appreciate how responsive his teachers have been.

We’re not looking to move him right now, but Melissa and I were curious about what it would look like if, in the future, we wanted to request a transfer to another South Washington County middle school—say Woodbury Middle School—if his schedule or activities change. Would that be an in-district transfer form, or would it require open enrollment paperwork if we stayed in ISD 833 but changed buildings?

Part of the question comes from trying to keep his stress manageable. Last time standardized testing ramped up, the MCA focus contributed to some pretty serious anxiety, and his pediatrician eventually prescribed sertraline. We’re trying to plan ahead so that his school environment stays supportive and doesn’t push us into any more medication changes.

If you have a link to the district’s “student’s district school” definition or policy, that would also be helpful. I just want to understand how his resident school (Lakeview) is determined and how hard it is to request a change if needed.

Thanks for your time,
Dan Carlson

metadata.subject: Question about transfer options within ISD 833 for Ethan

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Carlson <melissa.carlson@email.com>
To: enrollment@isd833.org
Date: August 6, 2025 9:18 AM
Subject: Clarification on Ethan’s enrollment – Lakeview vs. other district schools

Hello,

I’m double‑checking something in the parent portal for my son, Ethan J. Carlson (7th grade this fall). On one of the forms it asks for the “student’s district school,” and I want to be sure I’m entering it correctly.

Ethan is enrolled in South Washington County Schools (ISD 833) and physically attends Lakeview Middle School here in Woodbury. However, I’m confused because I see references to other buildings like Woodbury Middle School and Cottage Grove Middle School in some drop‑down menus, and I don’t want to accidentally select the wrong one if the form is actually asking for the *district* school rather than his *attendance* school.

For context, we are refusing his participation in the MCA tests this year based on recommendations from his therapist. During the MCA math testing window in April 2024 he had a full‑blown panic attack in his homeroom at Lakeview Middle School, and his therapist, Dr. Andrea Holm at Woodbury Pediatric & Adolescent Mental Health Clinic, formally diagnosed him with generalized anxiety disorder on May 6, 2024 and advised avoiding high‑stakes standardized testing for at least 12 months.

Could you please confirm what I should list as the “district school” for Ethan—should I just type “Lakeview Middle School (ISD 833)” or is there a specific district‑level designation you want us to use instead?

Thank you for your help,

Melissa A. Carlson
Parent of Ethan Carlson
7426 Willow Bend Drive
Woodbury, MN 55129

metadata.subject: Clarification on Ethan’s enrollment – Lakeview vs. other district schools

artifacts.content:
From: Dan Carlson <dcarlson@isd833.org>
To: Melissa Carlson <melissa.carlson@email.com>
Date: January 10, 2025 3:42 PM
Subject: Re: That testing refusal form

Hey Mel,

I looked over the draft you’re putting together for Ethan’s testing refusal and had a couple of thoughts about the section where it asks for the student’s district school.

Since Ethan is in 7th grade at Lakeview Middle School, which is part of South Washington County Schools, I’d stick with that language rather than listing any of the magnet or alternative programs you were considering. Don’t put anything about East Ridge High School in there either, even though that’s where I teach—different building, different level. The form is clearly about his current middle school placement.

If they want more detail, you could say something like “Lakeview Middle School – ISD 833 (South Washington County Schools).” I know you were wondering if you should instead reference the district office or one of the online learning academies, but that would just confuse things and make it look like he’s not actually attending a neighborhood middle school.

Also, your paragraph about last spring is strong. Maybe keep the part about April 9, 2024, when his anxiety escalated so much during MCA math that Ms. Ramirez had to call you at Medisphere HealthTech in the middle of your $250,000 client presentation, and your manager warning that another incident could affect your performance review. That really shows the impact on the family.

Let’s finalize this tonight after dinner.

– Dan

Title: ISD 833 Online Form – Verify Ethan’s School Info
Date: September 3, 2025 7:30 PM–8:15 PM
Location: Home office – Carlson residence

Description:

Block time to finish the back‑to‑school paperwork in the South Washington County Schools (ISD 833) parent portal for Ethan.

To‑do during this time:

1. Confirm Ethan’s enrollment shows Lakeview Middle School as his current building for the 2025–2026 school year.
2. Double‑check the “student’s district school” field in the assessment opt‑out form. The drop‑down list is confusing because it also shows Woodbury Middle, Oltman, and the online learning academy. Need to be certain we don’t accidentally pick the wrong school.
3. Paste in the updated refusal statement explaining that during the April 2024 MCA reading test, Ethan’s homeroom teacher at Lakeview, Mr. Jensen, told the class that kids who didn’t meet “proficient” could end up in remedial groups next year. That triggered Ethan’s long‑standing fear of being labeled like his cousin Tyler, who has an IEP for emotional‑behavioral disorders at Harding High School in St. Paul after a suspension in October 2022.
4. Verify Ethan’s grade level (7th), address on Willow Bend Drive, and emergency contacts.
5. Print a PDF copy of the completed form for our records and email it to Dan so he can see exactly what we submitted.

Reminder: Do NOT start any new applications for other districts or magnet schools tonight—this session is just for verifying existing ISD 833 / Lakeview information.

metadata.title: ISD 833 Online Form – Verify Ethan’s School Info

From: Enrollment Center <enrollment@sowashco.org>
To: Melissa A. Carlson <melissa.carlson@email.com>
Date: July 29, 2025 11:06 AM
Subject: Confirmation of 2025–26 Enrollment – Carlson, Ethan J.

Dear Parent/Guardian,

This message confirms that your student, CARLSON, ETHAN J., has been successfully enrolled for the 2025–2026 school year in South Washington County Schools (ISD 833).

Student Name: Ethan James Carlson
Grade for 2025–26: 7
Primary Attendance School: Lakeview Middle School – Woodbury, MN
Resident District: South Washington County Schools (ISD 833)

This email serves as verification for transportation routing, food service, and assessment records. When completing district and state forms that ask for your child’s “district school,” you should list the South Washington County Schools designation along with the specific building (e.g., “Lakeview Middle School – ISD 833”). Do not list your boundary high school (such as East Ridge High School) or any previous elementary school (such as Oak Meadow Elementary) in that field.

If your student participates in any shared‑time or online programs outside ISD 833, those programs should be listed only where specifically requested and not as the primary district school.

If any of the information above appears incorrect, please contact the Enrollment Center within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

ISD 833 Enrollment Center
South Washington County Schools
7362 East Point Douglas Rd S
Cottage Grove, MN 55016

artifacts.content:
Title: Meeting – Melissa & Counselor re: MCA refusal + school designation
Date: February 6, 2025 9:00 AM–9:45 AM
Location: Lakeview Middle School – Counseling Office

Description:

In‑person meeting between Melissa Carlson and Ms. Ramirez (school counselor) to finalize Ethan’s MCA refusal paperwork and clarify how his school is listed in district records.

Agenda:

1. Review Ethan’s history with MCA testing, including last year’s incidents.
2. Confirm that his primary building is Lakeview Middle School and that any forms asking for “student’s district school” should reference South Washington County Schools (ISD 833) with Lakeview as the site.
3. Ask whether listing the district office address versus the Lakeview building address makes any difference for how the opt‑out is processed.
4. Discuss how Ethan’s anxiety will be supported during other classroom assessments so situations like April 9, 2024—when his anxiety escalated so much during the MCA math session that Ms. Ramirez had to call Melissa at Medisphere HealthTech—are less likely to happen again.
5. Request written confirmation that Ethan will be provided an alternative learning activity during any future MCA windows instead of being placed in the testing room.

Participants:
- Melissa A. Carlson (parent)
- Ms. Ramirez (school counselor)

Bring: Copy of therapist letter from Dr. Holm, draft refusal statement, and Ethan’s current schedule showing all Lakeview Middle School classes.
